Display

New / moved wall Existing
Assumptions to verify
  • Bathroom 144″ × 93″, shower 36″ × 61″, vanity run 54″ × 97″, ceiling 93″ (vanity side) rising to 122″ at the window — from your sketch and notes. Bedroom size is estimated from photos (~12′ × 12′3″).
  • Bathroom door shown as a 30″ opening in the vanity-side wall, just left of the shower — confirm width/position.
  • West run laid out as: hallway entry (with bedroom door), walk-in closet mid-column, then the 97″ vanity + hall stretch ending at the sauna — approximate positions from your markups, confirm depths.
  • Bedroom, vanity area, and hall are fully open to each other except the curved wall's ~28″ tail, which the new shorter wall eliminates.
  • Linen closet shown at the west end of the north wall beside the built-ins.
  • The bathroom's own double-sink vanity (~46″, south wall next to the toilet) is shown staying in the same spot in the new design — confirm.
  • New straight wall assumed flush with the flat (bathroom) side of the old curved assembly → bathroom gains ~5″.
  • Bathroom ceiling now slopes north→south: high (122″) at the window/deck end, low (93″) at the hall end. The bathroom window is triangular, following that rake — tall at the north, tapering south.
  • Bathroom vanity kept at its original ~46″ length so there's comfortable standing room at the sink; freestanding tub sits alongside it. Confirm final vanity length and clearances.
  • Nook vanity is now a full-width makeup vanity (no plumbing): one big mirror, brass sconces, a center knee-space cutout, and a round stool. Brass here vs. black in the shower — confirm you're OK with mixed metals.
  • Shower, toilet, window, and sauna unchanged in both views.

Primary suite · reference renderings

Rendering mockups — the look we're going for

Photorealistic reference images of the intended finishes and mood — warm white-oak vanities, black hardware, arched mirrors. The 3D model is the to-scale layout; these show the feel.
